evals.name: generate-cross-platform-installers
evals.prompt: Create install.sh and install.ps1 for my GitHub Releases binary so users can install with curl/irm. It must verify checksums and work on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
evals.expected_output: Installer guidance/code that includes platform detection, release version resolution, checksum verification, archive extraction, install destination selection, and version confirmation.
expectations: Includes both install.sh and install.ps1 flows
Uses GitHub releases latest lookup with override option
Handles archive differences (.tar.gz for Unix, .zip for Windows)

evals.name: env-overrides-required
evals.prompt: Update my installer design so operators can use env vars to enable debug logging, set install directory, pin a version, and force reinstall.
evals.expected_output: Explicit environment-variable contract and behavior rules for both shell and PowerShell installers.
expectations: Defines env overrides for debug, install dir, version, and force reinstall
States version override bypasses latest lookup
States force override reinstalls even when binary exists
States install directory override takes precedence
Applies the same policy to install.sh and install.ps1

evals.name: path-remediation-prompt
evals.prompt: After install, if the binary directory is not in PATH, show a clear command the user can copy and paste based on their shell/profile file.
evals.expected_output: Post-install PATH verification and shell-specific remediation commands for bash/zsh/fish and PowerShell.
expectations: Uses command -v or equivalent on Unix and Get-Command on PowerShell
Provides shell-specific profile update command examples
Provides explicit PowerShell user PATH update command
Instructs user to restart shell or source profile after update
